title: [Feature]: Evaluate Oracle application connector families
problem: Oracle GoldenGate connectivity material lists Oracle application families such as Oracle E-Business Suite, Fusion Applications, NetSuite, Retail, Health, Transportation Management, and Utilities. nats-sinks does not yet have a policy for application-specific sinks versus generic HTTP, database, or event connectors.
proposal: Research whether Oracle application targets should become dedicated first-party sinks, documented integration blueprints, or examples built on generic HTTP, Oracle Database, object storage, or queue sinks. The evaluation should avoid over-specializing the core package while preserving a clear Oracle-centric path for high-value application integrations.
users: Oracle application owners, integration teams, and operators who need controlled event handoff into Oracle SaaS or enterprise application estates.
delivery_semantics: Each application family must define its own durable acceptance boundary before any ACK-gated sink can be implemented. Best-effort API calls must not be described as durable without proof.
security: Application credentials, tenant identifiers, business payloads, and endpoint URLs must be handled as sensitive. OAuth, API keys, mTLS, and least-privilege application roles must be documented for each future connector.
acceptance: Research classifies each Oracle application family as dedicated sink, blueprint, or out of scope.
The evaluation identifies shared security and testing requirements.
No application connector is implemented without a separate feature request.
Documentation avoids implying official Oracle application support beyond verified APIs.
Backlog items are created for any application connector recommended for development.
tests: Research-only item. Future connector tests must use API fakes, contract tests, and gated live tests with sanitized evidence.
documentation: Add findings to sink roadmap documentation and create future application-specific pages only when a connector is accepted.
closeout: Close after publication of the evaluation release and creation of any resulting feature requests.
